prompt: What fitness beliefs are you most against, relative to common wisdom?
expected_output: Greg pushed back on the premise that one unified 'common wisdom' exists across all fitness subcultures. His main concrete example was stretching: he argued static stretching is often unfairly maligned in lifting circles, despite strong evidence that chronic stretching can improve flexibility. He acknowledged acute pre-performance reductions can occur with very long/intense stretching immediately before maximal tests, but said those effects often dissipate after normal warm-up. He also used training frequency as an example of shifting consensus over time, showing how 'common wisdom' can reverse within a decade. So their broader answer was that context and subcommunity matter more than any single universal orthodoxy.
assertions: Answer captures the core SBS position on contrarian fitness views
Answer includes key specific details, numbers, or mechanisms mentioned by the hosts
Answer acknowledges important caveats or limitations discussed by the hosts
Answer does not contradict the hosts' stated position
